message:
docs(indexer): tighten cass#202 self-heal log target + report doc

Two small hygiene fixes from a third self-review pass:

1. The failure-case WARN in `run_index` was missing
   `target: "cass::fk_repair"` while the success-case WARN inside
   `cleanup_orphan_fk_rows` had it. Add the target so users filtering
   on `cass::fk_repair` see both halves of the lifecycle (success +
   "self-heal itself failed").

2. The `OrphanFkCleanupReport` doc still said "(or would have removed)",
   which was accurate when the WARN fired before the commit but is
   stale now that the function only returns the report after a
   successful commit. Replace with a precise note: the counts come from
   the count phase, not from `DELETE rows_changed`, and under the
   function's intended use (single startup pass holding the index run
   lock) those numbers match the rows actually removed plus any
   `ON DELETE CASCADE` descendants.

Pure documentation + log-target adjustments; no behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -8901,6 +8901,7 @@ pub fn run_index(
         && let Err(err) = storage.cleanup_orphan_fk_rows()
     {
         tracing::warn!(
+            target: "cass::fk_repair",
             db_path = %opts.db_path.display(),
             error = %err,
             "cass#202: orphan FK self-heal failed; continuing index run (cleanup will retry next pass)"

@@ -4423,7 +4423,14 @@ const ORPHAN_FK_TABLES: &[OrphanFkTable] = &[
     },
 ];
 
-/// Summary of what `cleanup_orphan_fk_rows` removed (or would have removed).
+/// Summary of orphan rows detected and removed by `cleanup_orphan_fk_rows`.
+///
+/// Counts come from the count-phase `SELECT COUNT(*)` rather than from the
+/// `DELETE`'s rows-changed return, so they reflect "orphans observed before
+/// the delete transaction started." Under the function's intended use (a
+/// single indexer-startup pass holding the index run lock), no concurrent
+/// writers exist and these counts match the rows actually removed plus any
+/// `ON DELETE CASCADE` descendants the engine reaped on top.
 #[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]
 pub(crate) struct OrphanFkCleanupReport {
     pub total: i64,
